however, the code itself does not include or execute any malicious logic.\n   - The reports do not claim or imply any supply chain attack or malicious payload.\n\n5. **Consistency & Reasonableness**:\n   - The scores are consistent across reports and appropriate for the code's nature.\n   - The confidence levels are all 1, indicating high certainty in the benign assessment.\n\n6. **Additional Considerations**:\n   - The code relies on external contract addresses; if those are malicious, the interaction could be harmful, but that is outside the scope of this code review.\n   - The code itself does not contain any malicious or obfuscated code.\n\n---\n\n### Final assessment:\n\n- **Malware Score**: **0** (no malicious code detected)\n- **Obfuscated Score**: **0** (clear, autogenerated code)\n- **Risk Score**: **0** (no vulnerabilities or malicious behavior)\n\n**Overall, the supply chain security incident appears to be a false alarm or a misunderstanding.
